name: Correct MMT-ADR-0020 + data-model §2B: drop over-claimed consent_request service-role RLS exceptions
description: MMT-ADR-0020 Consequences and docs/canon/identity/data-model.md §2B.1 claim consent_request ships named service-role RLS policy exceptions (public token-lookup + reminder-sweep). WI-780 investigation determined these are unnecessary and over-claimed: the app connects as the owner role (neondb_owner), which bypasses RLS (no FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, no app_user role-switch), and legacy consent_states — the posture canon says to match — carries no such exceptions. Doc-correction routed here from WI-780 (cutover lane must not edit the ADR/canon). Also carries the MMT-ADR-0020 ADR-hygiene rider (Deciders 'PM + Claude'; feat(database) PR birth).
acceptanceCriteria: In MMT-ADR-0020 Consequences and docs/canon/identity/data-model.md §2B.1 (Scope bullet): (1) DROP the claim that consent_request ships named service-role RLS policy exceptions for the public token-lookup and reminder-sweep paths. (2) REPLACE with a one-line statement of the actual service-role access model: 'Service-role consumers (public token-lookup, reminder-sweep) reach consent_request via the owner-role (neondb_owner) connection, which bypasses RLS — matching today's consent_states posture (no named service-role policy). A service-role policy exception is required only if/when the app_user role-switch cut-over (migration 0027 Phase 2-4) lands, at which point consent_request is swept with every other RLS table.' (3) Edit operator-signed, ADR<->canon lockstep, in a docs(adr) change-set per MMT-ADR-0000. Optionally fold the rider provenance fix (Deciders -> Architect (jjoerg) + PM, drop 'Claude'; re-date) in the same edit.
